title: Friction / slippage / commission stress (frozen engine, validation only)

cost_model.method: Each completed round-trip trade's realized r_multiple is reduced by a flat constant C expressed in R. Same C applied to every trade. No entry/exit/stop/target change; cost is applied to the already-frozen trade outcomes only.
cost_model.why_R_units: 1R is the strategy's own per-trade risk unit (stop = entry - 2*ATR20, so 1R = 2N). Expressing cost in R makes the deduction comparable across markets and trades without needing a dollar model. Converting a real per-trade $ cost to R requires dividing by the $ value of 1R for that trade; that depends on point value and ATR at entry and is NOT computed here -- these are conservative fixed-R scenarios.
cost_model.pf_expectancy_winrate_dd: recomputed on the cost-adjusted per-trade R sequence (chronological order preserved)
cost_model.break_even_cost_per_trade_r: base_total / n (the C that drives adjusted_total to exactly 0)

proxy_micro_descriptive.note: MNQ/MES are same-underlying micros, PARTIAL history (from 2019); descriptive only, NOT independent, cannot change the verdict. Their IS spans only the post-2016 trending era so their IS break-even cushion is much larger than the full-history primaries' -- NOT comparable to NQ/ES IS.

break_even_summary.note: Break-even C = the per-trade R cost that zeroes total R. The two FULL-HISTORY IS windows have the SMALLEST cushion (ES IS 0.0614R, NQ IS 0.0654R) -- the binding constraint. OOS cushions are 4-7x larger.

findings.oos_robust: BOTH NQ and ES OOS stay POSITIVE under every scenario including SEVERE (C=0.20R): NQ OOS +1.599R, ES OOS +6.058R at severe. OOS PF stays >1.1 throughout. OOS is robust to friction.
findings.is_thin_and_friction_negative: BOTH full-history IS windows have a thin edge that survives LOW and MODERATE friction but goes NEGATIVE at HIGH friction (C=0.10R): NQ IS -4.05R, ES IS -4.83R. By severe (C=0.20R) IS is deeply negative (NQ -15.75R, ES -17.33R) and IS max DD inflates (NQ 10.59->23.37R, ES 16.73->29.68R).
findings.is_break_even_tiny: The IS break-even cost is only ~0.06R on both primaries -- a very small cushion. Even modest per-trade friction materially erodes the IS edge.
findings.no_oos_negative: No hard-blocker condition fired: OOS does not go negative under low OR moderate (nor even high/severe) friction on either primary.
findings.micros_descriptive: MNQ/MES (post-2016-only IS) survive all scenarios on both windows, but their IS sits entirely in the favorable trending era so their large IS cushion is NOT comparable to the full-history primaries.

verdict_basis.not_robust_reason: FRICTION_ROBUST would require the full edge (incl. IS) to hold under high friction. Both full-history IS windows go NEGATIVE at high friction (C=0.10R): NQ IS -4.05R, ES IS -4.83R, with IS break-even only ~0.06R. The conservative call cannot certify ROBUST while IS flips negative under high friction on both primaries.
verdict_basis.not_fragile_reason: FRICTION_FRAGILE (IS or ES negative under MODERATE friction) did NOT fire: at moderate C=0.05R both IS windows are still positive (NQ +1.80R, ES +1.42R) and OOS is strongly positive on both.
verdict_basis.not_fail_reason: FRICTION_FAIL (OOS negative under low/moderate, or both NQ/ES fail) did NOT fire: OOS stays positive on BOTH primaries under every scenario including severe.
verdict_basis.sensitive_reason: Results survive LOW and MODERATE friction across both markets and OOS survives even SEVERE friction, but the thin full-history IS edge becomes negative under HIGH friction on both primaries -- the textbook 'survives low/moderate, weak under high/severe' pattern. OOS robust; IS fragile.
flags: IS edge is thin: break-even ~0.06R on both primaries -- smallest cushion in the study -- flagged.
Both full-history IS windows go NEGATIVE at high friction (C=0.10R) -- flagged.
IS max DD inflates sharply under friction (ES IS 16.73R -> 29.68R at severe) -- flagged.
OOS robust to severe friction, but OOS is the short post-2016 window already flagged in D15/D16 for single-regime dependence -- friction robustness does NOT cure regime concentration.

gate_decision.reason: The D14 hard blocker 'Negative OOS after realistic friction' did NOT fire -- OOS stays positive on both primaries through severe friction. FRICTION_SENSITIVE permits proceeding to S26-D18 final research-readiness review, carrying the thin-IS-edge friction fragility as an explicit caution. NOT a clearance to paper trade; D17 does not upgrade readiness.

trading_recommendation: NONE. No deployment, no paper, no live. S26 stays RESEARCH_CANDIDATE.
